  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Anchor bolts have been field-bent to fit the column base plate without engineering approval.",
    "Cold bending of anchor bolts can cause micro-cracking, reduce tensile strength, and compromise structural integrity."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Issue a non-conformance report (NCR) and halt further loading of the affected columns.",
    "Consult the structural engineer of record (SEOR) to determine if the bent bolts must be replaced, epoxied, or if they can be qualified by engineering analysis."
  ],
  "rationale": "Field-bending anchor bolts to force a fit is strictly prohibited by AISC 303 and OSHA 1926 Subpart R unless specifically authorized by the Structural Engineer of Record. Heating or cold-bending anchor bolts in the field compromises the metallurgical properties of the steel, creating a severe structural failure risk under tension or shear loads.",
  "references": [
    "AISC 303-16 (Code of Standard Practice for Steel Buildings and Bridges)",
    "OSHA 1926 Subpart R (Steel Erection)"
  ]
}
